name: Floating Island
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small mixing bowl, beat the egg white and cream of tartar until foamy.
2. Gradually add 2 tablespoons sugar (2 Tbsp) to the egg mixture, beating constantly until the sugar is dissolved (you can rub a bit of meringue between your thumb and forefinger to check), and the mixture is glossy and holds stiff peaks.
3. Heat milk in an 8-inch skillet or saucepan over low heat until it simmers.
4. Drop about 1/3 cup of the meringue mixture into the simmering milk, using a spoon.
5. Simmer uncovered until the meringues are firm, about 5 minutes.
6. Using a slotted spoon, remove the meringues from the milk and place them on paper towels to drain.
7. Set aside the remaining milk for custard preparation.
8. In the same or a medium saucepan, beat together eggs, egg yolk, 1/4 cup sugar, and salt until well combined.
9. Gradually pour in the reserved warm milk, stirring constantly until fully blended.
10. Cook over low heat, stirring, until the mixture thickens slightly and coats the back of a metal spoon.
11. Remove from heat and stir in orange juice concentrate and vanilla extract.
12. Pour the custard into a one-quart shallow serving dish or 9-inch pie plate.
13. Top with meringues.
14. Serve warm or chilled, and garnish each meringue with a small sprinkle of nutmeg if desired.
